hub:ecosystem.title: The Salish Sea
hub:ecosystem.body: The Salish Sea ecosystem — a living web of hundreds of interconnected species. Emergent clusters form from visitor dreams, revealing the themes that matter most to the community.

hub:artists.title: Artists & Witnesses
hub:artists.body: The artists and witnesses whose eyes, cameras, and brushes became the project’s sensory organs. Their work is the raw perception.

hub:training.title: Training Corpus
hub:training.body: 1,255 images across 50 species — the visual corpus that trained the Dreaming GAN. Every image is CC-licensed or used with permission.

hub:machine.title: The Dreaming Machine
hub:machine.body: The dreaming machine. Three projection layers: L1 = Moonfish footage cross-faded with ControlNet+LoRA style-transferred versions. L2 = StreamDiffusion real-time watercolor at 30fps. L3 = Raw Autolume GAN dreams. All composited in Resolume Arena onto the 8×8 ft wall.

hub:visitor-dreams.title: Visitor Dreams
hub:visitor-dreams.body: Your words become dreams. Speak or type your offering — the installation renders your vision as a living watercolor on the projection wall. Every dream joins the collective Dreamworld, where visitor voices cluster into emergent patterns.

hub:knowledge.title: Knowledge & Research
hub:knowledge.body: Reports, data, and Indigenous knowledge that ground the project. The Kwaxala model. Three-Eyed Seeing. The herring story.

hub:exhibition.title: The Exhibition
hub:exhibition.body: Digital Ecologies: Bridging Nature and Technology — Salt Spring Art Show at Mahon Hall, April 10–26, 2026.

concept:three-eyed-seeing.title: Three-Eyed Seeing
concept:three-eyed-seeing.subtitle: Western science + Indigenous knowledge + the land itself
concept:three-eyed-seeing.body: Three-Eyed Seeing integrates Western scientific observation, Indigenous Traditional Knowledge, and direct perception from the land itself. Not a hierarchy but a braiding — each eye sees what the others cannot.

install:digital-ecologies.title: Digital Ecologies
install:digital-ecologies.subtitle: Salt Spring Art Show — Mahon Hall, Apr 10–26, 2026
install:digital-ecologies.body: An interactive AI art installation exploring the Salish Sea ecosystem. The vision: not humans looking at nature through technology, but the Salish Sea using technology to perceive itself. Curated by Raf for the Digital Ecologies show.

person:briony-penn.title: Briony Penn
person:briony-penn.body: Naturalist, illustrator — watercolors and ecological storytelling. Her art is the visual language of the Salish Sea.

person:moonfish-media.title: Moonfish Media
person:moonfish-media.body: Underwater cinematography team capturing herring, salmon, and marine habitats. 13 videos, 416 frames used with collaborator permission.

person:david-denning.title: David Denning
person:david-denning.body: Photographer — long-term bioregional witnessing. 14 high-resolution photos used with collaborator permission.

person:eve-marenghi.title: Eve Marenghi
person:eve-marenghi.body: Data scientist, Regen Commons steward. HerringFest documentation.

person:carol-anne-hilton.title: Carol Anne Hilton
person:carol-anne-hilton.body: Indigenomics founder, vision holder. Framework for relational value and Indigenous economic design.

person:prav-pillay.title: Pravin Pillay
person:prav-pillay.body: Creative Director (MOVE37XR). TouchDesigner, AI visualization, immersive media. Studio: 108 Fraser Rd, Salt Spring.

person:darren-zal.title: Darren Zal
person:darren-zal.body: Systems architect. Built training corpus, gallery server, data map, and knowledge pipeline.

person:shawn-anderson.title: Shawn Anderson
person:shawn-anderson.body: Herring data science package — 339 files of herring stock assessment analysis.

person:raf.title: Raf
person:raf.body: Curator, Digital Ecologies exhibition at Mahon Hall.

person:brad-necyk.title: Brad Necyk
person:brad-necyk.body: Artist and researcher, latent space concepts.

artifact:dreaming-gan.title: Dreaming GAN
artifact:dreaming-gan.body: StyleGAN2-ada (320 kimg), ~1,255 images of 49 species. Abstract organic textures. Dual role: direct projection via Autolume + input to StreamDiffusion.

artifact:autolume.title: Autolume
artifact:autolume.body: SFU MetaCreation Lab. Real-time latent space navigation. Audio-reactive, OSC. Layer 3 of projection: raw GAN dreams. Also feeds StreamDiffusion as img2img input.

node:touchdesigner.title: TouchDesigner
node:touchdesigner.body: Integration hub. Autolume video via NDI, visitor prompts via OSC. Prompt Sequencer cycles 23 ecological prompts (26s each, 6s crossfade). StreamDiffusion runs inside as TOX.

artifact:streamdiffusion.title: StreamDiffusion
artifact:streamdiffusion.body: 30fps img2img with SD-Turbo inside TD. Takes Autolume GAN output + 23 cycling prompts. Layer 2: real-time watercolor via Briony LoRA. Visitor dreams interrupt the cycle for 30s.

output:projection.title: Projection
output:projection.body: The key node. 8×8 ft wall. Three layers in Resolume Arena: L1=Moonfish footage cross-faded with same footage through SD 1.5 + Briony LoRA + ControlNet depth (20 steps). L2=StreamDiffusion real-time watercolor. L3=Raw Autolume GAN.

node:gallery-server.title: Gallery Server
node:gallery-server.body: FastAPI server. Processes visitor text through GPT-4.1-mini for content filtering. OSC to TouchDesigner. SQLite for submissions. SSE for live updates.

node:qr-portal.title: QR Portal
node:qr-portal.body: Scan QR → phone browser → type/speak → 30s on projection wall.

artifact:briony-lora.title: Briony LoRA
artifact:briony-lora.body: LoRA rank 16, 22 watercolors, SD 1.5. Used in L1 (briony_video_style.py + RAFT smoothing) and L2 (StreamDiffusion real-time). Prompt: "brionypenn watercolor painting, Pacific Northwest coastal ecosystem".

td:osc-in.title: OSC In DAT
td:osc-in.body: Receives visitor prompts from gallery server

td:prompt-seq.title: Prompt Sequencer
td:prompt-seq.body: Cycles 23 ecological prompts (26s each, 6s crossfade). Visitor dreams interrupt for 30s with smooth fade.

cluster:briony-works.title: Briony Penn Archive
cluster:briony-works.subtitle: 519 paintings, illustrations, and field journals
cluster:briony-works.body: 519 paintings, illustrations, and field journals from Briony Penn’s decades of natural history work. Source material for the Briony LoRA style transfer model.

cluster:moonfish-footage.title: Moonfish Footage
cluster:moonfish-footage.subtitle: 13 underwater videos, 416 extracted frames
cluster:moonfish-footage.body: 13 underwater videos of herring spawning, salmon migration, marine habitats. 416 frames extracted. Layer 1 of the projection uses this footage cross-faded with ControlNet+LoRA style-transferred versions.

cluster:denning-photos.title: Denning Photos
cluster:denning-photos.subtitle: 14 high-resolution bioregional photographs
cluster:denning-photos.body: 14 high-resolution photographs of Salish Sea coastline, intertidal zones, forests. Long-term bioregional witnessing.

cluster:herringfest.title: HerringFest
cluster:herringfest.subtitle: HerringFest documentation — Eve Marenghi
cluster:herringfest.body: 65 documentary images from HerringFest on Salt Spring Island — community celebration of Pacific herring. Documented by Eve Marenghi.

doc:herring-report-i.title: The Salish Sea Herring
doc:herring-report-i.body: DFO management, shifting baselines, Indigenous rights, trophic cascade

doc:herring-report-ii.title: The Living Salish Sea
doc:herring-report-ii.body: Ecosystem health, Hornby stronghold, food web, conservation models

cluster:herring-data-science.title: Herring Data Science
cluster:herring-data-science.subtitle: Shawn Anderson’s data package — 339 files of herring analysis
cluster:herring-data-science.body: 339 files of Pacific herring stock assessment, DFO catch data, population models, and statistical analysis. Shawn Anderson’s quantitative foundation for the herring thread.

doc:digital-ecologies-book.title: Digital Ecologies: Mediating More-Than-Human Worlds
doc:digital-ecologies-book.subtitle: Turnbull, Searle, Anderson-Elliott, Giraud (eds.) — Manchester University Press, 2024
doc:digital-ecologies-book.body: The academic framework for the Digital Ecologies exhibition. Explores how digital technologies mediate more-than-human worlds across three sections: Digital Encounters (new human-nonhuman encounters through tech), Digital Governance (how tech reshapes environmental politics), and Digital Assemblages (entangled socio-technical-natural systems). CC BY 4.0. Key concepts: digital entanglement, technonatural present, pharmakon, glitches, ecomedia.

datasrc:inaturalist.title: iNaturalist
datasrc:inaturalist.body: Data source: CC BY / CC0. Images scraped, QC’d, and curated for the dreaming corpus.

datasrc:openverse.title: Openverse
datasrc:openverse.body: Data source: CC BY / CC0 / CC BY-SA. Images scraped, QC’d, and curated for the dreaming corpus.

datasrc:briony-penn.title: Briony Penn
datasrc:briony-penn.body: Data source: Artist permission — 54 watercolors. Images scraped, QC’d, and curated for the dreaming corpus.

doc:visual-politics-conference.title: The Visual Politics of Digital Ecologies
doc:visual-politics-conference.subtitle: Conference Programme — University of Oxford, Feb 2-3, 2026
doc:visual-politics-conference.body: Conference by the Digital Ecologies research group examining how emerging technologies create novel 'visual regimes' through which humans encounter the more-than-human world. AI fabricates 'hallucinatory ecologies,' digital twins offer impossible vantage points, VR layers and curates ecological attention. Visualization practices — capturing, compositing, rendering, mining, mirroring — carry political implications for precarious ecologies. Keynotes: Joanna Zylinska, Alexandra Daisy Ginsberg.

cluster:de-encounters.title: Part I: Digital Encounters
cluster:de-encounters.subtitle: New human-nonhuman encounters through digital technology
cluster:de-encounters.body: Part I of Digital Ecologies explores how digital technologies create new encounters between humans and non-human species. Covers exercise apps that surface wildlife (Running Wild), digital sound recordings and bioacoustics (Sonic Ecologies), conservation webcams (Trap-Cam of Care), and digital farming (Our Chicken Life). Key tension: do digital encounters displace or enhance in-person encounters with nature?

cluster:de-governance.title: Part II: Digital Governance
cluster:de-governance.subtitle: How digital tech reshapes environmental politics
cluster:de-governance.body: Part II of Digital Ecologies examines how digital technologies reshape environmental governance. Covers on-bird surveillance with albatrosses (animal-borne marine governance sensors), #AmazonFires social media activism (online composition of ecological politics), children's digital climate action, and collaborative seed data projects. Key question: who benefits from digitised environmental monitoring, and how does power operate through it?

cluster:de-assemblages.title: Part III: Digital Assemblages
cluster:de-assemblages.subtitle: Entangled socio-technical-natural systems
cluster:de-assemblages.body: Part III of Digital Ecologies explores entangled socio-technical-natural systems. Covers programming nature as infrastructure in Smart Forest Cities (Jennifer Gabrys), ecological computationality and more-than-human political actors (the 'dreaming mind'), and mediated natures as integrated analogue-digital frameworks. Moves beyond encounters and governance to fundamental co-constitution of digital, natural, and social.

de:ch01-running-wild.title: Running Wild: Digital Animals
de:ch01-running-wild.subtitle: Ch. 1 — Adams, Sandbrook, Tait
de:ch01-running-wild.body: Explores encounters with digital animals through exercise apps like Strava. How do running apps that surface wildlife sightings reshape human-animal encounters in the field? Examines the promises and pitfalls of gamifying nature encounters through digital platforms.

de:ch02-sonic-ecologies.title: Digital Sonic Ecologies
de:ch02-sonic-ecologies.subtitle: Ch. 2 — Hunter, Jasper, Prior
de:ch02-sonic-ecologies.body: Digital sound recordings mediating human encounters with non-human beings. Case studies: acoustic enrichment restoring coral reefs (Great Barrier Reef), the last Kaua'i 'ō'ō bird song, bioacoustics monitoring. Key concepts: digital sonic ecologies, the materiality of 'intangible' sound media, Big Tech's role in ecological data commodification.

de:ch03-trap-cam.title: Trap-Cam of Care: Lobster Webcams
de:ch03-trap-cam.subtitle: Ch. 3 — Remme
de:ch03-trap-cam.body: Conservation and the digital ecology of online lobster entrapment. Examines how webcams create care relationships between humans and non-human animals, and how digital visibility transforms conservation practices.

de:ch04-chicken-life.title: Our Chicken Life: Digital Flock
de:ch04-chicken-life.subtitle: Ch. 4 — Oliver
de:ch04-chicken-life.body: Byproductive labour in the digital flock. Explores how digital technologies mediate human-chicken relationships in farming, examining labour, care, and surveillance in digitised agricultural contexts.

de:ch05-on-bird.title: On-Bird Surveillance: Albatrosses
de:ch05-on-bird.subtitle: Ch. 5 — Davies, Lorimer
de:ch05-on-bird.body: Albatrosses equipped with digital sensors detect illegal fishing vessels — surveillance WITH animals, not just OF them. Introduces the 'movescape' (wet ontology of the ocean), biologging, and lively governance. Critical questions about naturalising surveillance as ecological solution and risks to enrolled animals.

de:ch06-amazon-fires.title: #AmazonFires: Online Ecological Politics
de:ch06-amazon-fires.subtitle: Ch. 6 — Gray, Bounegru, Colombo
de:ch06-amazon-fires.body: How the 2019 Amazon fires were composed as a political issue through social media. Analyses hashtag networks, viral image circulation, and the online composition of ecological crisis narratives. Examines how digital platforms shape environmental governance through public attention.

de:ch07-climate-action.title: Children's Digital Climate Action
de:ch07-climate-action.subtitle: Ch. 7 — McLean, Newman
de:ch07-climate-action.body: Children and young people's digital climate action in Australia. Explores co-belonging with place, ecology, and Country through digital activism and environmental education.

de:ch08-seed-data.title: Saving the Seed: Collaborative Data
de:ch08-seed-data.subtitle: Ch. 8 — Doyle, Dow
de:ch08-seed-data.body: 'Saving the knowledge helps to save the seed.' Generating a collaborative seed data project in London. Examines how digital databases mediate relationships between humans, seeds, and agricultural knowledge.

de:ch09-smart-forest.title: Programming Nature as Infrastructure
de:ch09-smart-forest.subtitle: Ch. 9 — Gabrys
de:ch09-smart-forest.body: Jennifer Gabrys examines the Smart Forest City in Cancún, Mexico — programming nature as infrastructure through sensors, data, and computational models. How forests become 'highly instrumented sensor spaces' for environmental governance.

de:ch10-computationality.title: Ecological Computationality
de:ch10-computationality.subtitle: Ch. 10 — Dwyer
de:ch10-computationality.body: Cognition, recursivity, and a more-than-human political actor. Explores ecological computationality — the idea that computation is not just a human tool but an ecological process. Relates to the 'dreaming mind' concept in the Salish Sea Dreaming installation.

de:ch11-mediated-natures.title: Mediated Natures: Analogue & Digital
de:ch11-mediated-natures.subtitle: Ch. 11 — Arold
de:ch11-mediated-natures.body: Towards an integrated framework of analogue and digital ecologies. Examines how forests are understood through different conceptual lenses and how digital and analogue media co-mediate human experiences of nature. Case study of Estonian forest harvesting.

concept:digital-entanglement.title: Digital Entanglement
concept:digital-entanglement.subtitle: Taffel — digital technologies entangled with more-than-human life
concept:digital-entanglement.body: Concept from Sy Taffel: the co-constitutive relationship between digital technologies and more-than-human worlds. Drawing from Guattari's three ecologies (mind, society, environment), Taffel shows how these ecologies are entangled through digital mediation. Deeply political — technologies can be repurposed towards just socio-environmental ends.

concept:technonatural-present.title: The Technonatural Present
concept:technonatural-present.subtitle: 'Natural' and 'technological' co-constitute each other
concept:technonatural-present.body: The current era where 'natural' and 'technological' do not make sense without each other. An assemblage of relations including human and non-human bodies, environments, and technologies. Digital ecologies scholarship finds progressive environmental politics in the 'glitches' of the technonatural present.

concept:pharmakon-media.title: Digital Media as Pharmakon
concept:pharmakon-media.subtitle: Simultaneously culprit and cure for ecological crises
concept:pharmakon-media.body: Drawing from Stiegler: the large-scale technical systems necessary for detecting, visualising, and mobilising around climate change simultaneously contribute to it. Digital technologies are situated, political, and affective mediators — not detached, neutral intermediaries.

concept:glitches-technonatural.title: Glitches of the Technonatural Present
concept:glitches-technonatural.subtitle: Generative fissures that enable alternative futures
concept:glitches-technonatural.body: Building on Legacy Russell's Glitch Feminism: moments of disruption in digitally mediated spaces that offer opportunities for alternative ecological futures. Glitches acknowledge 'the simultaneous ability for error and erratum' where 'each rupture offers an opportunity to correct for a different and better outcome.'

concept:ecomedia-cubitt.title: Ecomedia
concept:ecomedia-cubitt.subtitle: Cubitt — ethics of content AND material production of media
concept:ecomedia-cubitt.body: Sean Cubitt's concept: media designed to raise environmental consciousness. The ethics of ecomedia is only partially in the content — equally important is how content intersects with the material production of media itself. What is the ethical relation between a camera in an intimate ecological site and the affective imagery it produces?

concept:more-than-human-worlds.title: More-Than-Human Worlds
concept:more-than-human-worlds.subtitle: Decentring human exceptionalism, emphasising non-human agency
concept:more-than-human-worlds.body: Central framing of the Digital Ecologies book. Extends beyond animals to include plants, seeds, forests, ecosystems, and elements (water, clouds) that function as 'elemental media' (John Durham Peters). Places non-human life front and centre in questions about who benefits and who is excluded by digital mediation.

concept:three-ecologies-guattari.title: Guattari's Three Ecologies
concept:three-ecologies-guattari.subtitle: Mind, society, environment as inseparable
concept:three-ecologies-guattari.body: Félix Guattari's framework: the ecologies of mind, society, and environment are inseparable and must be addressed together. Foundation for digital entanglement theory. Maps to SSD's three-eyed seeing: Western science (social), Indigenous knowledge (mental/cultural), the land itself (environmental).
